title: Unresolved UAP Report, Gulf of Oman, June 2024
summary:
The United States Northern Command submitted a report of an unidentified anomalous phenomenon (UAP) to the All-domain Anomaly Resolution Office (AARO) consisting of 21 seconds of video footage from an infrared sensor aboard a U.S. military platform in 2024. An accompanying mission report, DoW-UAP-D8, described the UAP as consisting of an object with a vertical pole or bar attached to the bottom of the object. The observer also reported that the UAP may instead be a reflection from an object in the water.

Video Description:
00:00-00:21: An area of contrast visually resembling an inverted teardrop with a vertically linear trailing mass suspended below remains generally within the center of the sensor field-of-view throughout the video.

This video description is provided for informational purposes only. Readers should not interpret any part of this description as reflecting an analytical judgment, investigative conclusion, or factual determination regarding the described event’s validity, nature, or significance.
csv_raw_title: DOW-UAP-PR029, Unresolved UAP Report, United Arab Emirates, June 2024

audience.tldr: Department of Defense record from a military encounter report showing footage of a UAP encounter over Gulf of Oman.
what_we_dont: Exact date of the encounter is not specified in the released file.
Redacted portions may contain identifying or technical detail.
No kinematic data (speed, altitude, maneuver) is attached.
audience.mundane_candidate: A conventional explanation is technically possible but has not been demonstrated.
audience.score_sentence: Anomalousness Index 66/100. Top drivers: sensor quality (single_sensor_military), witness credibility (military_personnel).
